name: Warm inflation
summary: Inflation in which the inflaton field dissipates energy continuously into radiation throughout inflation, maintaining thermal equilibrium. Removes the standard cold-inflation requirement of a separate reheating epoch. The dissipative coupling provides thermal noise that generates curvature perturbations via fluctuation-dissipation theorem; predictions for n_s and r differ from cold inflation. Naturally avoids the eta problem of supergravity inflation.

addresses_puzzles.note: Thermal inflaton fluctuations replace quantum-vacuum fluctuations as the source of curvature perturbations. Predicts n_s ~ 0.95-0.97 and small r (in strong-dissipation regime); matches Planck data without fine-tuned cold-inflation potentials. No reheating epoch needed - dissipation populates the radiation bath during inflation.

touches_benchmarks.note: Specific thermal-noise dispersion relation modifies the curvature-perturbation power spectrum on top of slow-roll predictions; matches Planck n_s within slow-roll uncertainties.
Strong-dissipation warm inflation gives r << 0.01 (well below current BICEP/Keck bound).

introduces.note: Inflaton potential, dissipation coefficient Gamma, temperature T (set by Gamma).

history: Berera (1995), 'Warm inflation', Phys. Rev. Lett. 75, 3218 — established framework. Modern realisations: warm little inflaton (Bastero-Gil, Berera 2018+). Connections to swampland conjectures: thermal equilibrium can help satisfy trans-Planckian conjecture constraints.
